title: Vehicle Accident (Involved/Victim)
keywords: I was in a car accident
I am trapped in my car
steps: Check yourself and passengers for injuries. Do NOT move if you suspect a spinal injury.
If possible, turn off the engine and turn on hazard lights.
If safe to do so, exit the vehicle and move away from traffic to a sidewalk or shoulder.
If trapped, stay calm and wait for the 'jaws of life' or emergency rescue.
Call emergency services and provide your exact location and vehicle description.
Do NOT attempt to move your vehicle if it is badly damaged or in a dangerous position.

title: Vehicle Accident (Witness/Bystander)
keywords: I just saw a car crash
reporting an accident on the highway
there is a car wreck ahead
debris from a crash on the road
steps: Pull over in a safe location away from the accident. Do NOT block emergency access.
Call 911/112 immediately. Provide the highway mile marker or nearest intersection.
Describe the number of vehicles involved and if you see smoke or fire.
Approach the scene only if it is safe. Do NOT become a second victim in traffic.
If victims are conscious, talk to them and keep them calm until help arrives.
Provide your contact information to the police as a witness.

title: Pedestrian Struck (Victim/Driver Involved)
keywords: I was hit by a car
vehicle hit me on the crosswalk
driver hit me and kept going
I am bleeding in the street
steps: For the pedestrian: Do NOT try to get up. Stay still to avoid further injury.
For the driver: Stop immediately. Do NOT move the vehicle unless it is a direct hazard.
Call emergency services and specify that a pedestrian was struck.
Check for breathing and pulse. If you are trained and the victim is bleeding, apply pressure.
Keep the victim warm and talking. Do NOT move their head or neck.
Wait for paramedics and provide an honest account of the event.

title: Pedestrian Struck (Witness)
keywords: I saw a pedestrian get hit
steps: Protect the scene. Use your vehicle or other objects to block traffic from hitting the victim again.
Call 911/112. Provide the location and a description of the striking vehicle (if it fled).
Stay with the victim. Do NOT move them unless there is an immediate fire risk.
Direct traffic or ask other bystanders to help clear the area for the ambulance.
Note the time of the incident and any details about the driver's behavior.
Stay until the police arrive to provide your statement.

title: Vehicle Fire (Driver/Passenger)
keywords: smell of burning plastic in car
my electric car is venting smoke
steps: PULL OVER safely and turn off the engine immediately.
GET OUT and move at least 100 feet (30 meters) away from the vehicle.
DO NOT open the hood if smoke is coming out; oxygen will cause a flare-up.
STAY AWAY from the rear of the car (fuel tank area).
CALL emergency services once you are in a safe location.
Do NOT return to the vehicle to retrieve personal items.

title: Vehicle Fire (Witness)
keywords: I see a car on fire
smoke from a parked car sighting
gas leak catching fire on road
steps: Call 911/112. Provide the location, vehicle description, and if it's blocking traffic.
Maintain a safe distance; vehicle fires produce toxic smoke and can explode.
Warn other drivers to stay back using your hazard lights.
If the driver is still inside, signal them to get out from a safe distance.
Do NOT attempt to put out a large engine fire with a portable extinguisher.
Stay on the line to update dispatch if the fire spreads to nearby vegetation or structures.

title: Bicycle Accident
steps: Ensure the cyclist is in a safe location away from moving traffic.
Do NOT remove the helmet unless it is obstructing the airway.
Check for responsiveness and bleeding.
Immobilize the head and neck if you suspect a spinal injury.
If the cyclist was wearing a helmet that took an impact, it must be replaced (it is no longer safe).
Collect witness information and photos of the scene if possible.

title: Public Transit Incident (Train/Bus)
steps: If on a train, stay away from doors and windows during impact.
Be aware of high-voltage 'Third Rails' or overhead lines; do NOT touch the tracks.
Locate emergency exits and hammers (if needed to break glass).
Follow the driver's or conductor's instructions immediately.
If you must evacuate onto tracks, watch for other moving transit vehicles.
Help others evacuate if safe to do so.

title: Submerged Vehicle (Trapped Inside)
keywords: my car is sinking in a lake
I am underwater in my car
steps: SEATBELTS: Unbuckle yourself and any children immediately.
WINDOWS: Open or break the side windows. Do NOT try to open the doors.
OUT: Exit through the windows. Push children out first.
SURFACE: If you cannot open the window, wait until the car is full of water, then the door will open.
SWIM: Follow the air bubbles to reach the surface.
SIGNAL: Once on shore, signal for help and check for hypothermia.

title: Submerged Vehicle (Witness)
keywords: I saw a car drive into the water
witnessing a car go off a bridge
witnessing person trapped in sinking car
car swept away by current witness
steps: Call 911/112 immediately. Provide the exact location and if you see people inside.
Note the spot where the car went down; use landmarks (trees, poles).
Do NOT jump in unless you are a trained water rescuer; the current or suction may pull you down.
If the victims surface, throw them anything that floats (ropes, coolers, tires).
Keep a visual on the area to help divers find the vehicle later.
Wait for emergency services and describe the number of passengers you saw.

title: Brake Failure (Driver)
keywords: brake pedal went to the floor
steps: PUMP the brake pedal hard and fast to build up any remaining pressure.
DOWNSHIFT: Move the gear selector to a lower gear to let the engine slow you down.
PARKING BRAKE: Apply it gradually and steadily. Do NOT jerk it (prevents skidding).
ALERT: Turn on hazards and honk the horn to warn other drivers.
ESCAPE: Look for an open field, uphill slope, or runaway truck ramp.
IMPACT: If you must hit something, aim for the softest object (bushes, guardrail) at an angle.

title: Brake Failure (Reporting Sighting)
keywords: I see a car out of control
car speeding with no brake lights
car speeding through red lights uncontrolled
reporting car with no brake lights speeding
steps: Maintain a safe distance from the out-of-control vehicle.
Call 911/112 and provide the car's description, license plate, and direction of travel.
Warn other drivers by using your own hazard lights or flashing high beams.
Do NOT attempt to block or stop the runaway vehicle with your own car.
If the vehicle crashes, pull over safely and provide assistance as per 'Vehicle Accident Witness' protocol.
Stay on the line with dispatch to provide live updates on the vehicle's location.

title: Tire Blowout at High Speed
keywords: loud pop and loss of control
steps: Grip the steering wheel firmly with both hands to maintain control.
Do NOT slam on the brakes. Hard braking will cause you to lose control of the vehicle.
Gradually ease off the accelerator to let the car slow down on its own.
Correct the steering to stay in your lane; the car will pull to the side of the blowout.
Once you have regained control and slowed down, signal and pull off to a safe spot.
Turn on your hazard lights and call for roadside assistance; do not change the tire on a busy highway.

title: Stuck Throttle / Unintended Acceleration
steps: Shift the vehicle into NEUTRAL (N). This will disconnect the engine from the wheels.
Apply the brakes firmly and steadily. Do NOT pump them (loses vacuum assist).
Steer the vehicle to a safe location off the road.
Once stopped, turn off the engine. Do NOT turn it off while moving (loses power steering/breaks).
Check if the floor mat is interfering with the pedal.
Do not drive the car again until it has been inspected by a professional mechanic.

title: Wrong-Way Driver (Avoidance)
keywords: a car is coming at me in my lane
car driving opposite direction toward me
wrong way car in my lane
car coming at me fast wrong direction
almost hit by wrong way driver
steps: Slow down and move to the RIGHT immediately. Do NOT pull to the left shoulder.
Flash your lights and honk your horn to alert the wrong-way driver.
If a collision is unavoidable, steer toward a ditch or soft shoulder.
Stay in your vehicle once stopped; the wrong-way driver may have caused secondary crashes.
Call 911/112 once you are in a safe position.
Do NOT attempt to turn around and chase the wrong-way driver.

title: Wrong-Way Driver (Sighting/Reporting)
keywords: I just saw a wrong way driver
reporting a car going the wrong way
reporting car on wrong side of highway
wrong way driver at exit ramp
high speed wrong way driver sighting
saw car enter highway the wrong way
steps: Safely pull onto the right shoulder immediately.
Call 911/112. Provide the exact location (mile marker, exit) and vehicle description.
Warn other drivers using your hazard lights and flashing high beams.
Note the license plate only if it can be done without putting yourself at risk.
Stay on the shoulder until you are sure the danger has passed.
Do NOT follow the wrong-way driver to get more information.

title: Stuck in Traffic / Gridlock Emergency
steps: If you are stuck for a long period in extreme heat, turn off the engine to prevent overheating, but stay inside if it is safer.
Stay hydrated. Keep emergency water in your car for these situations.
Keep your doors locked and windows up if the area is unsafe or congestion is due to civil unrest.
Monitor local radio or GPS apps for the cause of the delay and estimated clearance time.
Do NOT use the shoulder for passing unless instructed by emergency personnel; the shoulder is for emergency vehicles only.
If you have a medical emergency while stuck, call 112/911 and provide your exact coordinates and car description.

title: Wildlife/Animal on Road (Hazard Reporting)
keywords: reporting a bear on the road
steps: Slow down and use hazard lights to warn drivers behind you.
Maintain a safe distance. Do NOT approach or try to shoo the animal yourself.
Call the non-emergency traffic line or 112/911 if the animal is a major hazard to high-speed traffic.
Provide the exact location (highway, mile marker, or intersection) and direction.
If the animal moves off the road, continue with caution as others may follow.

title: Wildlife / Livestock Collision (Post-Impact)
keywords: hit a cow with my car
struck a horse on the road
bleeding animal in the street after crash
steps: Pull over safely to the shoulder or a safe area.
Turn on your hazard lights and stay in the vehicle if traffic is heavy.
Do NOT approach the animal. Injured animals are unpredictable and dangerous.
Call 112/911. Report the collision, vehicle damage, and if the animal is blocking lanes.
Check your vehicle for fluid leaks or safety hazards (broken lights, dragging parts).
Document the scene for insurance once it is safe to do so.

title: Autonomous Vehicle System Failure
steps: Grip the steering wheel immediately and prepare to take manual control.
Apply the brakes steadily to override any automated acceleration or erratic movement.
Disengage the autonomous system (via button or brake pedal) and steer to a safe location.
Check for sensor obstructions (dirt, ice, or damage) that may have caused the failure.
Report the system failure to the vehicle manufacturer and local traffic authorities.
Do NOT re-engage the autonomous mode until the system has been professionally inspected.

title: Road Rage (Victim/Targeted)
keywords: angry driver trying to run me off road
steps: DO NOT GO HOME. Drive to a police station, fire house, or busy public area.
KEEP WINDOWS UP and doors locked at all times.
AVOID EYE CONTACT and do not respond with gestures or shouting.
IF BLOCKED, honk your horn continuously to attract attention and call 911.
RECORD: If safe, use a dashcam or phone to record the license plate and driver.
GIVE SPACE: If they try to cut you off, slow down and let them pass.

title: Road Rage (Witness)
keywords: cars swerving at each other reporting
steps: Maintain a safe distance from the aggressive vehicles.
Call 911/112. Provide descriptions of both vehicles and their license plates.
Report if you see any weapons or if the drivers have exited their vehicles.
Do NOT intervene or try to mediate the conflict yourself.
Note the direction of travel and the nearest exit or landmark.
Provide your contact information as a witness to the authorities.

title: Medical Emergency (Driver)
keywords: I am feeling faint while driving
sudden chest pain at the wheel
I'm having a stroke while driving
steps: PULL OVER IMMEDIATELY to the shoulder or nearest safe spot.
PARK: Put the car in Park (P) and turn on hazard lights.
UNLOCk: Unlock the doors so responders can reach you.
CALL: Dial 911/112 or use your SoterIA SOS button immediately.
LEAN BACK: Recline the seat if you feel you might lose consciousness.
STAY PUT: Do NOT try to drive yourself to the hospital.

title: Medical Emergency (Passenger/Witness)
keywords: driver is slumped over the wheel
my driver is having a heart attack
witnessing car swerving and driver unconscious
calling for aid for driver in distress
steps: FOR PASSENGERS: Grab the steering wheel and guide the car to the shoulder. Shift to Neutral (N) and apply the parking brake gradually.
FOR WITNESSES: Call 911/112. Report a 'driver in distress' or 'incapacitated driver'.
Describe the vehicle and its behavior (swerving, stopped in lane).
If the car is stopped, check for a pulse and breathing. Perform CPR if trained and necessary.
Look for medical ID bracelets or signs of diabetic shock (insulin, glucose).
Keep the victim's airway clear until paramedics arrive.

title: Level Crossing Emergency (Stalled on Tracks)
steps: GET OUT IMMEDIATELY. Move everyone out of the vehicle even if you don't see a train.
MOVE TOWARD THE TRAIN: Run at a 45-degree angle AWAY from the tracks but TOWARD the direction the train is coming from (to avoid flying debris).
LOCATE ENS: Look for the Blue Sign (Emergency Notification System) and call the number provided.
GIVE USDOT NUMBER: Provide the 6-digit number and 1 letter on the sign to identify the crossing.
CALL 911: If no ENS sign is visible, call 911 and specify you are on railroad tracks.
DO NOT attempt to push the car unless you have a clear view of miles of track and a second person to watch.

title: Level Crossing Emergency (Witness)
keywords: witnessing train approach vehicle on rails
steps: DO NOT go onto the tracks. Stay at a safe distance from the crossing.
Call the emergency number on the blue ENS sign at the crossing immediately.
Provide the USDOT crossing number from the sign to the operator.
If you see a train approaching, signal the driver by waving your arms low (if safe) and move far away from the potential impact zone.
Call 911/112 to report the obstruction and any victims who may still be near the tracks.
Do NOT attempt to tow or push the vehicle yourself if a train is potentially inbound.

title: Downed Power Lines on Vehicle (Inside)
keywords: sparks hitting my roof power line
steps: STAY INSIDE. The car is insulated by the tires. You are safe as long as you don't touch the ground and the car at the same time.
CALL 911 and the utility company immediately. Warn others to stay back at least 35 feet (10 meters).
IF FIRE STARTS: Jump clear of the car. Do NOT touch the car and the ground simultaneously.
LAND WITH BOTH FEET together. Do NOT take steps (voltage difference between feet can kill).
SHUFFLE AWAY: Keep both feet on the ground and shuffle or hop away with feet together until you are 35 feet away.
DO NOT return to the vehicle for any reason.

title: Downed Power Lines on Vehicle (Witness)
keywords: saw power line fall on car
reporting downed wires on road vehicle
bystander reporting trapped driver live wires
dangerous electrical line crossing road witness
steps: STAY BACK at least 35 feet (10 meters) from the vehicle and the lines.
CALL 911 and the utility company. Specify that there is a person trapped inside the vehicle.
DO NOT approach the car to help the victim; you could be electrocuted by the ground current.
YELL to the victim to stay inside the car until the power is confirmed cut by the utility company.
Keep other bystanders away from the area.
Note the utility pole number if visible from a safe distance.

title: Tunnel Fire/Emergency (Trapped Inside)
steps: STOP: Pull to the side. Turn off the engine but leave the KEY in the ignition (for rescue crews).
EVACUATE: Exit the vehicle immediately. Do NOT try to retrieve belongings.
EMERGENCY DOORS: Look for illuminated green running man signs and enter the emergency exit or cross-passage.
STAY LOW: If there is smoke, stay close to the floor where the air is cleaner.
HELP: Use the emergency SOS phones inside the tunnel (they provide exact location to operators).
WALK AWAY from the smoke/fire toward the nearest exit.

title: Tunnel Fire/Emergency (Witness/Reporting)
keywords: traffic stopped at tunnel entrance fire
steps: DO NOT ENTER the tunnel if you see smoke, red lights, or a closure sign.
CALL 911/112. Report the specific tunnel name and direction of travel.
PULL OVER outside the tunnel entrance to clear the path for emergency vehicles.
Listen to emergency radio broadcasts (usually on specific FM frequencies for the tunnel).
Warn other drivers behind you to stop or turn around safely.
Provide information on how many vehicles you saw entering before the smoke started.

title: Child / Pet Locked in Hot Vehicle
keywords: locked keys in car with baby
steps: Call 112/911 immediately. State: 'Child trapped in hot vehicle'.
If the child is unresponsive or in distress, you may be legally protected to break a window away from the child.
Shade the vehicle if possible.
Stay with the vehicle until responders arrive.

title: Road Obstruction / Debris Reporting
keywords: furniture in the middle of the road
steps: Slow down and turn on hazard lights as you approach the obstruction.
If you can safely pass, do so and then pull over to a safe location.
Call 112/911. Provide the exact location and describe the size/type of obstruction.
Do NOT attempt to move large or heavy objects yourself if there is high-speed traffic.
Warn other drivers if you have road flares or a warning triangle (place at least 50m before).

title: Vehicle Breakdown / Stalled on Roadway
keywords: broken down in middle of road
steps: Turn on hazard lights immediately.
If the vehicle is still moving, try to coast to the shoulder or nearest exit.
If stuck in a live lane, do NOT exit the vehicle if traffic is high speed. Stay buckled.
Call 112/911 and state: 'Stalled vehicle in live lane of traffic'.
If you can safely exit to the shoulder, move away from the vehicle and stand behind a guardrail.
Call a towing service once emergency responders have secured the scene.
